entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A highly nutritious cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) grown for grain production in arid and semi-arid tropical regions.

entities.name: GRAIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The primary purpose for the cultivation of pearl millet.

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Agroecological regions characterized by low rainfall where pearl millet is adapted.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is grown, with India being the largest producer.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is grown.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The largest global producer of pearl millet.

entities.name: CROSS POLLINATION
entities.description: The natural pollination mechanism of pearl millet.

entities.name: SINGLE-CROSS HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids produced by crossing two inbred lines, typically yielding more than open pollinated varieties.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of agricultural productivity of the crop.

entities.name: OPEN POLLINATED VARIETIES
entities.description: Crop varieties that are pollinated naturally, used as a baseline for hybrid yield comparison.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic system used to facilitate the production of hybrid seeds.

entities.name: HYBRID DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The breeding process used to create high-yielding hybrid varieties.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which develops hybrid parental lines.

entities.name: HYBRID PARENTAL LINES
entities.description: Specific lines bred to serve as parents in the creation of pearl millet hybrids.
